reference.title: Documentation Quality Pack
reference.description: Guidelines for maintaining high‑quality documentation as part of the repository. Documentation is as important as code; it must be correct, complete, easy to navigate and accessible. This pack describes how to build, lint and validate documentation across languages.

section.title: Minimum documentation files
section.content: Every repository should include a `README.md` explaining the purpose, setup, usage, contribution guidelines and licence. Include a `CONTRIBUTING.md` to describe the development workflow, a `CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md` for community expectations, and a `SECURITY.md` with vulnerability disclosure instructions. For specifications or policies, maintain an ADR directory and index.

section.title: Building documentation
section.content:
Use a documentation generator appropriate to the language:
**Python** – Sphinx or MkDocs with autodoc. Configure `conf.py` to pull docstrings and type hints.
**JavaScript/TypeScript** – JSDoc, TypeDoc or Docusaurus for component libraries. Use Storybook for UI components.
**Rust** – Rustdoc (`cargo doc`).
**Go** – `godoc` or `go doc`.
**Java** – Javadoc via Maven or Gradle. Generate a site with Asciidoctor or Antora if needed.
**.NET** – DocFX or Sandcastle.
Build docs in CI using the `ci-docs-quality.yml` workflow. Fail the build if documentation generation fails.

section.title: Linting and proofreading
section.content: Use tools like markdownlint, vale, alex and cspell to check Markdown files for style, inclusive language and spelling errors. Fix issues reported by these tools. Use link checkers (e.g., `markdown-link-check`, `mkdocs build --strict`) to ensure that all cross‑references work.

section.title: Keeping documentation in sync with code
section.content: When code changes affect APIs or behaviour, update the docs in the same pull request. Use comments or annotations (e.g., Python’s docstrings or TypeScript’s JSDoc) as the single source of truth so that documentation generators remain accurate. Add documentation tasks to the definition of done.
